reviews.review_description:
The meadows is my favorite map but, I can't relax when I keep getting jump scared by the deer who clip into my body because there is no collision. There's a menu where you can plant 5 trees max, plant butterflies, change from night to day, change the weather to rain, plant rocks, and plant seeds that attract wildlife which doesn't always work. 

The maps just read lazy to me because the most interesting thing to look at is the skybox. The trees are too sparse and everything just seems sort of randomly placed. The dinosaur map would be awesome if you could actually go up to a dinosaur but they are only to look at through an invisible barrier. The deep ocean one with the blue whale honestly is just nightmare fuel because it's eerily empty besides a few sunken statues, some dolphins whom you can't interact with, and a scary blue whale that  lingers somewhere in the darkness. They should have done a coral reef instead, but that would probably be more work.

I'll come back when you get to pet the animals, there are more woodsy areas to explore, the movement speed becomes faster, an option to turn the animals off the map, and if the deep ocean  becomes one of the two: A magically beautiful corral reef or commit to the treacherousness of it all and go full blown scary creepy nightmare ocean. It's Already halfway there.
I think this is amazing. I can lay down and be calm and float around space or the ocean... WOW

I sat on a hill on a mountain, and watched a family of deer wander by. I tossed “food” on the ground in front of me, and the deer came close enough to feel like they were on my lap. I made the rain start falling while I hung out with the birds and butterflies and bunnies watching the deer snack.

This game is absolutely what I was searching for, it is the embodiment of relaxation, and if you’re thinking about getting it, I sincerely suggest you do!! My favourite part is the Jurassic world, it blew my mind away and I LOVE dinosaurs, seeing pterodactyl’s soaring over my head up in the sky was an amazing experience, and all of the sounds of nature really immersed me into it! I love the rain feature, the sound of rain falling + the sky thundering is perfect for taking my mind off of anything, I adore this game already!
Even though it isn't best visually, I will give 5 stars because of great experiences. I especially like rain mode option and its tranquility.  Interactivity in app is more than good. Nice ideas.
I haven’t even played it yet (before writing this) and I can already tell this will be one of the most chill VR games or even one of the most chill games in general ever!

reviews.review_title: So far the best nature app
reviews.review_description: Gives a really nice immersive nature experience. Would encourage them to add more landscapes!

As a massive nature lover, I gave this 4 stars out of 5, purely for the concept and to encourage the developers to keep their focus on this project.
But I will also be realistic, this game is pathetic, and I apologize if it hurts feelings. It is essentially a few worlds, where most are worlds that are almost replicas of others, and it is not realistic, at all.
I used the quest game enhancer and used the hd mode for this game, and it still wasn’t enough, the original game without quest enhancer is beyond blurry and too cartoonish which murders the vibe completely.
I have been into programming before, and have been gaming for 25 years+, so here is my advice to make it much, much better.

First and foremost, this is Quest, the ONLY, and I mean ONLY thing people want here , is to feel immersion and think they are actually there, so focus on realism, a lot.

That alone will fix 70% of the issues, and no its not “we are limited to quest 3”, plenty of games here where the immersion makes you question reality.

Second, add more active nature. This should be self speaking, but add interactions with animals, or at the very least, add animals doing things, or having a pet animal with you, that you interact with also.

It seems like a lot of work, but once you do this, you will be able to justify selling this for 40$+, and plenty of people would buy it also.

Very nice.  I do feel the butterflies are rather dull looking and very small. Though realistic, for me it would enhance the experience if they were larger, and different shapes and colors. I would love a paradise waterfall option, with humming birds, parrots, and larger multi-colored butterflies.

I thoroughly enjoy this app. It is really restful. You have to manage your expectations are little. Sometimes animals seem to bump into each other and on one I was doing just now he kept head-butting stones but it is fun. I enjoy bringing up to change environment.

I love this app, but judging by some of the reviews it is a victim of its own misdirected marketing. It's called Nature Treks, but you can't actually trek anywhere as the arenas are fairly limited in area. The trailer makes it out to be an exciting and lively construction type game, but this bears no relation to the actual experience. Maybe these ideas were in the original plans, but this is not the app which is delivered.

So what is it and why do I love it?

It is a gorgeous and calming app of meditative environments. Pick a scene, go hang out and relax for a bit. It does this very well. There is some interaction available, but really if this is what you're after you're going to wear it out fairly quickly. Just find a nice spot and chill 

If I love it, why only 4-stars?

A couple of small tweaks would make a huge difference. We are given the option to change between day or night, between dry or wet weather. But having to make the decision and do this manually breaks the spell. Implementing these changes on some sort of automated rotation would be so much better. I appreciate this would require some sort of transition for day night, but if this is too much even a quick fade to black then back again would be infinitely better than no automation. Add in some optional guided meditation, rebrand it as a meditation app and you would have a pretty solid product.

Additional thought since the update with the boundary mesh: Pllease add an option to switch this off. It's unnecessary and completely destroys the immersion. I much preferred how it was before.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the lovely review. 
I've taken note of the suggestions about the time of day. A transition option is possible to implement in the future I think. By the way I adding an off toggle for the boundary message.

reviews.review_description:
Update: It's wonderful the devs listen to their fans of this app. You now can remove the perimeter wall and message in the settings so the nature immersion is back! Thank you ! However it would be wonderful if you could put the full version of the game like it is on the Rift on the Quest 2 or 3. They both can handle it now that stand alones havemore processing power and memory. Thanks! 
Love this meditative and beautiful game!
 I love the surroundings  and beautiful landscapes. I was so  amazed at first I started crying happy tears! I love how you control the environment.  The rainstorm had me so relaxed I almost fell asleep.  Well worth the reasonable price.  Great for first time VR users or older folks not into game play. All around beautiful and awesome experience! But all around a great relaxing and experience

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sticking with it and waited for the update to fix the perimeter message.

More content is on its way.

reviews.review_description: I used this app for meditation and it really helps. however I am looking for some more scary places where there are surely no human such as abandoned graveyards, abandoned villages. I hope the update will make it

Updates and expansion packs! Take my money please! Other than that I’m loving everything In here, though the animals sketch me out a little when they get close. It would be nice to have a little shelter and fire on all maps to watch from a distance like you have on a few of them. Or perhaps, even the option to have a platform in the trees of sorts. More locations though via paid expansion pack would be awesome. Maybe even some larger locations in a completely separate expansion pack. 

Seriously though, this app is one of my
Favourites on the quest store. Great graphics for what it is.

developer_response.body:
This a really nice review, thanks so much.

I have shelters to more environments and will continue to do this.

Additional environments will definitely happen and will be part of the continued release of free updates. The animal models and also behaviour is also being worked on.

reviews.review_title: Totally why I got a VR headset
reviews.review_description: Sure you can see the lines from time to time but I'm in it for the feelings it gives me,  the relaxation and wonder.   Hoping they add a few more portals to explore. IMO worth the money I spent.

reviews.review_title: A need for development changes & update
reviews.review_description:
It would be greatly beneficial if they introduced updates that incorporate new features designed specifically for fitness enthusiasts. Imagine the possibilities of tracking workouts like running on a treadmill or cycling, with the ability to set and achieve personal goals. This could inspire users to stay motivated and feel a sense of accomplishment by earning rewards for their progress. 🏆


The environments they create are remarkably lifelike, brimming with intricate details that capture the essence of reality. From the vibrant colors of nature to the subtle nuances in lighting and shadow, every element contributes to an immersive experience that feels authentic and engaging. 🌴

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for the wonderful review and thoughtful feedback. Integrating into fitness tech is a great idea. - John

reviews.review_description:
2025 update 
there have been improvements and additions, the perimeter wall was a good idea but you have to give the option to disable it thanks. 

the animals are still the weak point, the eyes need to get better currently all the animals' eyes appear lifeless. 

the animations are very bad. I can see a deer coming near me and spinning on itself as if it were on some kind of carousel that makes it spin like a rigid puppet. 

the same Deer walks with indifference and crosses entering into interpenetration with tree trunks and rocks. it totally ignores the obstacles. 

on the island where there are palm trees, they swing a bit too much, since not only the upper part but also the entire lower trunk swings it seems unrealistic, so if you can't swing only the upper part of the palm tree trunk then reduce its movement, make them move less, thanks 

all in all if I really had to choose one important point in which I would really like you to focus are the animations, thank you very much. 

are you a single developer or a team? could I communicate directly with you please? do you have a discord channel?



2023 update
please instead of adding new environments, improve animals, animations, fix penetration bugs, make the a.i. of animals slightly better, correct the wrong proportions of some animals (for example gray parrot too small) correct the eyes of the animals, make them alive, shiny. focus on this. improve certain details of the environment, such as the stream with too many edges, or the sea that is not beautiful

----

Update :

however it is not tolerable that in 2022 you do not update the app with significant improvements on the graphics and animations of the animals, quest 2 allows it and we have now seen its potential, I am also willing to pay a nice update but I do not need you to put new scenarios and introduces new bugs with animals that continue to be unsatisfactory. as soon as it came out I gave it 5 stars for a long time but now it should evolve

Update:

but sorry who works for the graphic and color department? a color blind? especially in the autumn environment the colors are wrong, the old version had the right colors. are visible wrong. animals need to be improved, eyes lifeless, kangaroo mouth too red inside, this app may be 5 star amazing but there are trivial mistakes that spoil it, animals crossing plants, crossing each other them, who do not detect collisions, graphics especially of the eyes and other parts to redraw, the foliage of the trees of australia is to be improved the color, overall the map is beautiful as well as many other maps, practically almost all of them, but go to review the colors and bring them back to the old version and improve a lot of the animals, the prehistoric map is also very beautiful, the trex if you call it does not come like the other dinosaurs, it stays far away and you can't see it well. even the lonely tree on the beach of the new map needs to be slightly improved, the little boat is too white, it doesn't look good from a distance. some animals are off the scale such as the gray parrot, other birds fly in a strange way, the animations need to be improved, thanks for your attention

--
Update 2
but don't you see that in "red fall" you have given a bluish color to the trunks of the trees? are they horrible, when you restore the old original color? also from the initial menu where you see the image of the layer to be selected you can see the old color of the trunks and also of the foliage that you have made a brighter orange, these colors are wrong, is it possible that you do not shorten them? adding content is of little use if you make mistakes and don't correct past ones. do we talk about tigers? tigers that graze the grass like cows? the gray parrot should be at least twice its current size, the gray parrots are much larger
----
---

the last update ruined the experience, the colors are wrong, especially the tree trunks in some scenes, in the other quest I keep the old version and it's much better as color tints, plus the new dinosaur settings have different bugs and animals in the other maps keep moving strangely, they have unrealistic and dull eyes, some like zebras have red eyes, there is still some work to be done on the animals and to bring the scenery colors back to more color correct

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the updated review. I will add an option to disable the perimeter.

Edit: The most recent update now has a toggle in the settings to remove the perimeter wall and message.

reviews.review_description: I really like the app. Great variety of landscapes, beautiful features like rain, night/day switch and calming music and sounds.

This captures the feeling of the places that it takes you to. You can make it rain or snow, day or night. A nice escape. I am glad that there are people who are so creative that they can make apps like this.

developer_response.body:
It's really great when I read a review from someone who gets the intention of the experience. I hope it brings you many more peaceful escapes.
New content will be coming soon.

reviews.review_description:
Thank you for add turn on and off space alerts  setting.

It's a good app, Should get 5 stars if you can set turn on and off space alerts. Because it blocking the beautiful view. Before the update, there is no warning. This app was beautiful, but it was destroyed by latest update.

developer_response.body:
You are very welcome, thank you for being a supporter of the application.

I do always take note of feedback and maintain a doc where I work through things. Updates will continue and I am working towards a significant general upgrade with some fundamental improvements, changes and additions.

reviews.review_description: Terrible

developer_response.body: Thanks for giving it a go. If you liked the concept and have some suggestions for improvements I am always willing to listen and add new things.

reviews.review_title: Wanted a quiet place to sit with a view
reviews.review_description:
It’s so simple: just several nice environments where you can sit quietly and enjoy by yourself, with some nature sound, some animals… What’s more is that you can adjust day and night, making it look drastically different in some of them. This was exactly what I was looking for. I’m not a mediation kind of person, but this does fall in line with meditation apps. The environments all have their ability to go from day to night, add rain, add some rocks or trees or seeds. You open the menu, and can throw balls of orbs to achieve all of this. It’s quite beautiful and very serene. Some of them have some surprises in them when you switch from day to night or vice versa. Like the humongous sunset in the Red something (I forget). I won’t ruin other surprises (don’t expect dramatic surprises, just something like, “cool, I did not expect that” kind of surprises). But my favorite is White Winter’s night time. The sky is gorgeous. Sometimes I add some snow, and I’ll just lie down and stare at the sky for 10 min at a time and reflect on my day or let my mind wander.

The one thing I did not anticipate was the ability to reach out and to break some twigs and look at it. Look for the glowing/flying things around the twigs, bcs not everything is touchable. Sometimes, there would be birds or butterflies that would fly onto the twigs I’m holding. So, I’d use my other hand to sort of pet it. Other than picking up/breaking off the twigs, there’s no haptics on your hand/controllers when you touch things, but sometimes I can’t help doing it anyways. This was a really nice touch. NOTE TO CREATOR: would be great to add some haptics to reach out and touch some of the environments (I know; too much to ask, but this app deserves it!).

The quality is pretty good to above average, to be honest. It’s hardly as memory-intensive like Brink is, so that’s kind of expected. If you look up at the trees, the individual leaves are not going to be clear, for example. As an environment as a whole though, it is rather beautiful, to say the least. I absolutely would pay extra for better quality though. Even DLCs. But I suspect that may not happen. 

The trekking part is limited to an area. So it’s not open-world, so don’t expect that due to its name. 

Overall, I absolute love this app. This is exactly what I’ve been looking for. It’s my most used app so far, even on the Quest 3, bcs I just use it to unwind.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for this review. I can see so much thought has gone into it. Some more updates are due so I have made a note of your suggestions. - John

reviews.review_description:
This is amazing  you can go into and get emersed in different landscapes 
Explore   create and add to your environment  or just sit and enjoy the peace watching the different  birds and animals

developer_response.body: Thank you for your review and feedback, it's genuinely appreciated so much.

reviews.review_title: The best thing on VR headsets. 5 stars!
reviews.review_description:
The Nature Treck VR truly fulfills my dreams for a a use of VR headset. It transports me to places I can’t visit, allowing me to observe and almost touch nature. It’s so relaxing and beautiful. This “game/experience” has the potential to be the best thing on VR headsets. 5 stars!

If I can offer any suggestions, the graphics are already fantastic. Making them even more realistic would be a game-changer!

I appreciate the smaller worlds, but adding more things to watch, like in David Attenborough programs, would be delightful. Birds building nests, mice eating grains—these simple and cute scenes would enhance the experience.

Incorporating more sounds, such as the delicate wind, river, or waterfall, would add depth and beauty.

The possibilities for expanding this experience are limitless. I sincerely hope you continue to develop it, making it even more stunning. Good luck!

developer_response.body: This is really good feedback, thank you. It's all very possible to do (Adding more realism is challenging) so I will see what can be implemented in future updates.

reviews.review_description:
I would give it 5 stars if it had been updated recently, the graphics are dated (wow that is a thing now in VR) other apps like ocean rift are far superior (though that is just the ocean of course) 

I hope at some point they decide to give it a face lift.

12/9: an update was done and you can certainly see the improvements! I wish I was able to bump this up to 4.5 stars. This gets me excited for the future of this title.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review. A face lift is in the process as you have mentioned. Nature Treks has allot of diverse environments compared with Ocean rift that does one thing. This does present some challenges to catch everything. I will continue to try and raise the bar.

reviews.review_description: I love a new enviroments and chill in VR. Sadly Meta don't made a new enviroments for Quest more often

developer_response.body: Hi Tom, Thanks for the great review. I'm working on a new update that will have some new environments. I also want to re-work the existing environments. Its going to be allot of work but I think it will be worth it for the extra immersion.

reviews.review_description: You can only "trek" about the distant of your Meta home, sometimes less. Graphics are not at all crisp, can't interact with anything. Waste of money garbage.

developer_response.body: Sorry you did not get anything from the app. The app currently has 15 environments to explore with more on the way. - John

reviews.review_description: You can only move in a small area. The animations are poor. I was ready to delete the app in five minutes.

Well they got the creeptastic feeling down to a T. Sadly you can only move a few feet before the cursor turns red and blocks you from going where the action is.

Want to see a brontosaurus up close or that T-Rex? Nope! Stay in your little box with the crow that makes the same sound over and over and there's no way to shut it up. Those dinos are half a mile away.

I did seem to break out of the box on the creepy underwater one, though. The ring of statues blocks you, but I saw a crashed plane.

Then there was the level where I saw the deer. It walked right up to me. Other than that it was pretty full, but maybe I'll tell you about that time I saw a deer again.

Overall it feels like it should be a free app to get people to want to pay to leave the box and actually go somewhere to explore in the game. The paid version could have all levels meshed into one world, and add caves and other fun stuff.

developer_response.body: Hello, thanks for the feedback. I had a look at the crow! He should pester you less now. The underwater environment has no wall, you are free to explore. Same for the space environment. Unfortunately this is not possible yet with the exterior environments as they are more demanding on the hardware. I agree it would be lovely to open them up at some point. I hope you manage to find more animals in the environments besides the deer, there are lots to find.

reviews.review_description: Not really what I was expecting and feel like I just wasted $10.
Would it have killed you to make the waves hitting the beach look real?  I bought it for the beach experience only.  It’s horrible.

Pointless rubbish, no idea how anyone is leaving a 5* review. It's like a poor quality cartoon with nothing happening and nothing to do. The best bit is being able to make it rain. I wanted to be drifted through a realistic forest soaking it all in - but nope, this is not the app for that.
Amazing felt as if I been transported, so relaxing x

I know what this app is trying to do, and in some ways it succeeds, but, in others it falls very short.

The environments available are lovely, but you're incredibly limited in how much you can explore them. There's a very narrow window in which you can move, which is a real shame. The app's called Nature Treks, but there's no trekking at all. If I could wander off and maybe find something that no-one else had found previously, then that would've been great. Instead, I'm limited to moving in an area that might be 100m^2, and that's me being generous. I feel it would've been better to have fewer environments, but allow you to explore each more.

Additionally, the animation for a number of the animals is unconvincing. Many look like they're not even connected to the ground while they walk, which breaks the illusion significantly.

Having said that, I do enjoy the ability to control the environment, and my 5 year old daughter loves the game - although she gets very frustrated with the limited window of movement mentioned earlier. If she sees an animal off in the distance, often she can’t even reach it. 

In short, it's okay, but I was hoping for a lot more than what I got - mostly due to the lack of any real trekking. I rarely feel the need to just jump into a trek, which is a shame. Hitting that invisible movement barrier so quickly just breaks the app for me.

Totally disappointed. I have the Rift version and the difference is huge. Small stages, fewer environments, I bought it thinking that for quest 2 it would be the same as in rift, but there is no comparison, much worse .. Especially in Meadows, a small circle, surrounded by a river, you can hardly move, well, I have not liked anything. Instead, the Rift version, I love it.The excuse that quest does not have the same power as rift is not valid for me, I have games that are practically the same in one version as in another.

I was a little disappointed that almost every game had the same items of choice to make your experience amazing. There was 3 that had the exact same items to grow or create.
I got to the Last one as I was enjoying them all, BLUE MOON. 
I WAS THEN HORRIFIED AND COMPLETELY DISMAYED. I WENT FROM A FEELING OF WELL BEING AND JUST AWESOMENESS. TO MAJOR PTSD, EMOTIONAL SUFFERING AND MAJOR HEARTACHE. 
I LOVED THE IMAGE OF THE BLUE MOON OVER THE WATER. I LOVED THE MYSTERIOUS WINDMILL. AS I LOOKED AT MY CHOICES OF CREATIONS. THERE WAS A NEW ON NOT IN ANY OF THE OTHERS. I WAS HAVING TROUBLE FIGURING OUT JUST WHAT IS WAS.
THEN TO MY HORROR AS I BENT DOWN TO TAKE A LOOK AT THIS SMOOTH TOP TYPE OF STONE. I WAS INSTANTLY TRAUMATIZED BY THE FACT THEY WERE GRAVE STONES. WTH WERE YOU THINKING PUTTING THIS IN AN APP. THAT IS SUPPOSED TO BE RELAXING, MEDITATING AND JUST AN OVERALL GOOD FEELING.
MY DAUGHTER WAS KILLED 4 YEARS AGO AND THE SEVERE SUFFERING EVERYDAY GRIEF FROM THAT IS ONE OF THE MAIN REASONS. I CHOSE YOUR NATURE TREK. MY GOOD FEELINGS THAT I HAVE NOT BEEN ABLE TO ACHIEVE ALTHOUGH I HAVE TRIED OTHER MEDITATIONS AND SUCH. INSTANTLY TURNED TO A GUT WRENCHING KNIFE IN MY HEART. I WAS ABSOLUTELY MORTIFIED.

developer_response.body:
Hello, each environment is open to interpretation, this is how they were designed. The rock shapes bear no clear identifiers, they are plain rocks with a curved top. 
I'm genuinely unable to see the cause for this level of distress, I am sorry you are going through a delicate time. Everything I do in Nature Treks is with a positive intent, it always has been and always will be. John

reviews.review_description: This app has the best immersive environments I've seen so far. The nighttime lighting is so realistic, I truly get lost in the sense of wandering around outside at night. Interactions are nice but not necessary for me. Perfect for escape or meditation, which has been so valuable during the past year. Look forward to some guided meditation audio in future versions?

I love everything about this app but please allow us to unlimited trekking rather than being stopped at a certain boundary. Also let us touch and feel the animals and add more themes, areas and sceneries. Please don’t stop improving and it’s amazing. Love the music and sound affects.

I just spent an hour scuba diving, wandering through forests with rabbits & deer, floating through the universe and more. This is truly as relaxing as meditation. Kids will also love it, as you can feed the animals, plant flowers, throw stars, you name it. Definitely get this one for the chill factor.

reviews.review_title: Relaxing, great for new VR players
reviews.review_description:
Personally enjoyed this game for its purpose. It was nice to play it in a quiet room, imagining being there at the scene and listening to the music. I appreciate the additional effects and meditation circle. 
Sadly I do wish there was more elements to the game, or least more variants in ways to interact between environments.
